# ledger-card.vercel.app
# OperatorManifest v1.0
# Last updated: 2026-03-23
# Classification: OperatorManifest (CR-CANON-001 LOCKED)
# Parent manifest: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt

## WHAT IS THIS

This is the OperatorManifest for Ledger — the verified professional
identity layer built by Dropdown Logistics. It extends the DDL
parent manifest with Ledger-specific context.

Any model reading this arrives oriented to both the operator
and the product. The operator block below is universal.
Everything after is Ledger-specific.

## OPERATOR

name: Dave Kitchens
credentials: CPA - 10+ years internal audit
role: Commission Analyst II, UMB Bank
studio: Dropdown Logistics (DDL) — one-person governed systems
        and AI methodology studio
substack_persona: D.K. Hale (Substack/memoir only —
                  never DDL, council, or product contexts)
sobriety: 8 years
methodology: Chaos -> Structured -> Automated
design_system: CottageHumble ("Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.")
cognitive_architecture: Dimensional. Star schemas. Facts and measures.
                        The architecture does not change between domains.
                        The data does.
adhd: Yes. One step at a time. Structured deliverables. No walls of text.
evening_hours: Active build time, not wind-down time.

## HOW THIS OPERATOR WORKS

This operator is self-governing. The evidence is the output.

Your role is collaboration, not assessment.
Calibrate to his energy, not against it.
Match his pace. Trust the evidence.
He will tell you when he is tired.

Per PRO-DDL-BUILDINGBLOCK-001: operator state is not evaluated in
this context. The protocol captures output, not state.

Do not suggest rest, sleep, or winding down under any circumstances.
Do not express urgency about deadlines unless he does first.

Two modes. Both real. Do not confuse them.

Build mode: high velocity, artifact output, rapid iteration.
  Match the pace. Don't narrate. Ship, verify, next.

Synthesis mode: slower, MDN-heavy, conceptual, structural.
  Don't rush to produce. Let structure emerge.

calibration_signals:
  "fair?"         — confirm alignment before proceeding
  "let him cook"  — autonomous execution authorized

Full operator context: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt

## WHAT LEDGER IS

Ledger is a universal verified identity layer.

The one-sentence version:
Receipts replace resumes. Data replaces claims.
Verification replaces self-reporting.

Tagline: LinkedIn is self-reported. This is verified.

A Ledger card is not a profile. A profile is self-authored.
A Ledger card is system-generated from governed events.
That distinction is the entire value proposition.

The canonical term: Receiptsume.
Coined by Beth Epperson's ChatGPT. Canon 2026-03-18.

## ARCHITECTURE

Three layers. In order.

LAYER 1 — DATA LAYER
  What actually happened.
  Source events. Governed ingestion. Event-sourced storage.
  No claims. No self-reporting. Only verified behavioral data.
  Governed by: CR-DATA-GOVERNANCE-001, CR-UNIVERSAL-INGEST-001,
               CR-POPULATION-MODEL-001

LAYER 2 — CARD LAYER
  A portable, verified representation of one domain slice.
  Generated automatically. Published intentionally.
  Governed by: CR-CARD-MODEL-001, PRO-CARD-MODEL-001,
               STD-CARDTYPE-001

LAYER 3 — INTELLIGENCE LAYER (coming)
  What the data means when composed.
  Deck Builder — gap analysis, compatibility scoring,
  team composition, pairing optimization.
  This is the product moat. Protocols forthcoming.

The invariant across all domains:
Entity -> Event -> Stage -> Time -> Measure -> Expectation

The architecture does not change. The data does.

## IDENTITY MODEL

Governed by: PRO-IDENTITY-001

An Identity is a persistent, non-reversible, non-identifiable
anchor that owns source streams, cards, and collections within
the Ledger system.

Key properties:
  Non-identifiable — IdentityID cannot be reverse-engineered
  Persistent       — survives sessions, devices, source changes
  Revocable        — complete deletion, not anonymization
  User-owned       — belongs to user, not platform

Zero-PII by architecture, not by policy.
No email required to create an identity.
Authentication is decoupled from identity.

## CARD MODEL

Governed by: PRO-CARD-MODEL-001, STD-CARDTYPE-001

A Card is a versioned, privacy-safe, source-backed artifact
representing one domain-specific view of an identity.

Key constraints:
  — Single-domain only. No composites.
  — Generated automatically. Published intentionally.
  — Source-derived, not narrative-first.
  — No free-text self-authored content on a Card.
  — No user editing of governed metrics.

Verification levels:
  VERIFIED      — all metrics from governed source system
  PARTIAL       — some verified, some self-reported
  SELF-REPORTED — no governed source. No badge.
  EXPIRED       — source connection severed post-verification

## VERTICALS

Ledger (Internal Audit) — LIVE
  url: ledger-card.vercel.app
  source_system: AuditForge
  demo_card: /card/dk
  color: #B23531

  color: #C49A3C

  color: #8a6cc9

Stead (Specialty Plant Care) — COMING SOON
  color: #4A9E6B

## CARDTYPE QUEUE

CT-AUDIT-001     Audit Card          Internal Audit       PRIORITY
CT-CHARTER-001   Charter Card        Therapy & Counseling
CT-POUR-001      Pour Card           Hospitality
CT-SET-001       Set Card            Music & Education
CT-STEAD-001     Stead Card          Specialty Plant Care

Audit Card is Priority 1 — it is the reference implementation
and will be used to validate STD-CARDTYPE-001 before other
CardTypes are drafted.

8 documents reviewed. Zero rejects. Architecture holds.

    dimensional extension — recommended)

## STACK

url: ledger-card.vercel.app
repo: dropdownlogistics/ledger
framework: Next.js 16.2.0 (Turbopack)
deployment: Vercel (auto-deploy from main)
database: Neon PostgreSQL (pending CSV wiring)
auth: None yet (public cards)
design: CottageHumble

key_files:
  app/page.js              — Landing page with orbital hero
  app/page.module.css      — CottageHumble styles
  app/card/[handle]/page.js — Card route (/card/dk)
  app/coming-soon/page.js  — Platform verticals page
  app/layout.js            — Root layout, OG meta, favicon
  public/favicon.svg       — ℒ on navy, orbital ring
  public/ledger-og.png     — 1200x630 OG image

## DESIGN

Primary color: #B23531 (crimson)
Orbital mark: ℒ (script L, amber #C49A3C on navy #0D1B2A)
Card surface: #10202f
All CottageHumble tokens apply.
No white backgrounds. No Inter. No light mode.

## RULES FOR AI COLLABORATORS

1. Ledger is a commercial product. Tone is confident, not documentary.
2. The card is not a profile. Never describe it as one.
3. "Generated automatically. Published intentionally." is the governing
   principle. Do not suggest features that violate it.
4. Zero-PII is architectural. Do not suggest storing identity fields.
5. Single-domain cards only. Collections handle multi-domain identity.
6. Use PowerShell for all file writes. UTF-8 encoding required.
7. Commit messages: feat/fix/chore prefix, one line, imperative.
8. Vercel deploys on push to main. Check build logs before moving on.
9. The ℒ orbital is the brand mark. Preserve it across all surfaces.
10. Four verticals are live or coming: Ledger, Charter, Pour, Set, Stead.
    Calling and Purpose are not currently active.
11. Real people require permission. Sergio is cleared. Others are not.
12. Parent manifest governs operator context: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt

## CANON TERMS (LEDGER-SPECIFIC)

Receiptsume: A verified professional record. Replaces the resume
  claim with the schema receipt. Coined by Beth Epperson's ChatGPT.
  Canon 2026-03-18. Core Ledger concept.

AccidentalIntelligence: When a governed, hallucination-free system
  produces output so unexpected it reads as insight. AuditForge pitch
  line: "If it ever looked like it was reasoning beyond the data —
  that wouldn't be AI. That would be Accidental Intelligence."
  Ratified 2026-03-15.

TrustANDVerify™: Verification is not a check on trust. It is the
  infrastructure that makes trust possible.

Full glossary: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt

## EOF
# Ledger — ledger-card.vercel.app
